commands.repair_reason: Contributor-supplied comparison proof is still needed, and any decision to drop the layout contracts is a maintainer review call rather than an automated repair.; Cleared: The diff only touches WinUI XAML layout and related tests; I found no concrete security or supply-chain concern. commands.review_summary:
The PR moves Diagnostics page padding inside the MaxWidth=900 MainView and DetailView content containers, trims DebugPage XAML layout comments, and removes three Diagnostics page-width contract tests.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence runtime reproduction was established in this read-only review. Source inspection gives a medium-confidence path: Diagnostics pads outside the 900px cap while Permissions pads inside it, which should affect wide-window card width.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-supplied comparison proof is still needed, and any decision to drop the layout contracts is a maintainer review call rather than an automated repair.

target.title: Fix Diagnostics page width to match Permissions
target.body: The Diagnostics page column was slightly wider than Permissions because padding was on the outer `ScrollViewer`/`Grid` while `MaxWidth=900` was on the inner content container — so cards filled the full 900px. Permissions puts `Padding=24` inside the `MaxWidth=900` `StackPanel`, making cards 852px wide. ## Changes - Move `Padding=24,24,24,24` inward onto the `MaxWidth=900` content containers in both MainView and DetailView so the column matches Permissions exactly. - Trim verbose layout comments in `DebugPage.xaml`. - Remove three diagnostics-only XAML contract tests (`DebugPage_PageDimensionsMatchPermissions`, `DebugPage_WrapsCenteredContentInStretchingContainer`, `DebugPage_DetailViewMatchesMainViewPadding`). No other page has equivalents. ## Validation - `./build.ps1` ✅ - Shared tests: 1891 passed / 29 skipped ✅ - Tray tests: 1175 passed ✅ - Manual: Diagnostics column now visually matches Permissions on wide windows.
target.author: bkudiess

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
